New Brunswick, N.J. (January 24, 2023) – Johnson & Johnson (NYSE: JNJ) today announced results for fourth- quarter  and  full  year  2022.

678bf327-4664-401a-8683-e6318af35696.label: text_chunk

“Our  full  year  2022  results  reflect  the  continued  strength  and  stability  of  our  three business segments, despite macroeconomic challenges ,” said Joaquin Duato, Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer. “I am inspired by our employees who make a difference in the health and lives of people around the world every day.

  As we look ahead to 2023,

Adjusted operational sales* reflected below excludes the net impact of acquisitions and divestitures and translational currency.

f6c0c085-9291-4c33-8f32-1de691aec817.label: text_chunk
f6c0c085-9291-4c33-8f32-1de691aec817.text:
Consumer Health

Consumer Health worldwide adjusted operational sales increased 3.9%* predominately driven by over-the- counter (OTC) products.  Major contributors to growth in OTC were TYLENOL and MOTRIN analgesics, as well as upper respiratory products and digestive health products in the international markets.  Additionally, Skin Health/Beauty adjusted operational growth was primarily driven by NEUTROGENA outside the U.S.

e5879afe-5798-4f6b-a0b1-5fe69d3c5af4.label: text_chunk
e5879afe-5798-4f6b-a0b1-5fe69d3c5af4.text:
Growth was partially offset by Oral Care in the international markets.

Pharmaceutical

Pharmaceutical  worldwide  adjusted  operational  sales  grew  6.

8%*,  driven  by  DARZALEX  (daratumumab),  a biologic for the treatment of multiple myeloma, STELARA (ustekinumab), a biologic for the treatment of a number of  immune-mediated  inflammatory  diseases,

634f3b12-ec8b-4b11-be4a-afdab60859b7.label: text_chunk
634f3b12-ec8b-4b11-be4a-afdab60859b7.text: ERLEADA  (apalutamide),  a  next-generation  androgen  receptor inhibitor for the treatment of patients with prostate cancer, TREMFYA (guselkumab), a biologic for the treatment of adults living with moderate to severe plaque psoriasis, and for adults with active psoriatic arthritis,

36859e49-515b-4161-87f6-23c24ac69bf4.label: text_chunk
36859e49-515b-4161-87f6-23c24ac69bf4.text: and INVEGA SUSTENNA/XEPLION  and  INVEGA  TRINZA/TREVICTA  (paliperidone  palmitate),  long-acting,  injectable atypical antipsychotics for the treatment of schizophrenia in adults.

fe9017c7-6793-4bc1-888b-c384134d4854.label: text_chunk
fe9017c7-6793-4bc1-888b-c384134d4854.text: This growth was partially offset by declines in  sales  of  REMICADE  (infliximab),  a  biologic  approved  for  the  treatment  of  several  immune-mediated inflammatory diseases, IMBRUVICA (ibrutinib), an oral, once daily therapy approved for use in treating certain B-cell malignancies, a type of blood or lymph node cancer, and ZYTIGA (abiratone acetate), an oral, once daily

1113fedd-43ba-4632-a2ba-d9ecd0308482.label: text_chunk
1113fedd-43ba-4632-a2ba-d9ecd0308482.text:
medication for use in combination with prednisone for the treatment of metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.

MedTech

MedTech worldwide  adjusted  operational sales  grew  6.1%*,  driven  primarily  by  electrophysiology  products in
Interventional Solutions, contact lenses in Vision, and wound closure products in General Surgery.

7a3a1f14-72db-4f83-9de3-bfdb76d627b2.text:
ABOUT JOHNSON & JOHNSON:

At Johnson & Johnson, we believe good health is the foundation of vibrant lives, thriving communities and forward progress. That’s why for more than 135 years, we have aimed to keep people well at every age and every stage of life. Today, as the world’s largest and most broadly-based health care company, we are committed to using our reach and size for good.

0fabf929-9675-48e4-8328-0010b1b34bd4.label: text_chunk
0fabf929-9675-48e4-8328-0010b1b34bd4.text:
We strive to improve access and affordability, create healthier communities, and put a healthy mind, body and environment within reach of everyone, everywhere. We are blending our heart, science and ingenuity to profoundly change the trajectory of health for humanity.

This press release contains “forward-looking statements” as defined in the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995 regarding, among other things: future operating and financial performance, product development, market position and business strategy, and the anticipated separation of the Company’s Consumer Health business. The reader  is  cautioned  not  to  rely  on  these  forward-looking  statements.

  If  underlying  assumptions  prove  inaccurate  or  known  or  unknown  risks  or uncertainties materialize, actual results could vary materially from the expectations and projections of Johnson & Johnson.

to patents; the impact of patent expirations; the ability of the company to successfully execute strategic plans;

Acquisition, integration and divestiture related for 2021 primarily includes the gain on the divestiture of two Pharmaceutical brands outside of the U.S.European Medical Device Regulation (MDR) costs represent one-time compliance costs for the Company’s previously registered products.

 MDR is a replacement of the existing European Medical Devices Directive regulatory framework, and manufacturers of currently marketed medical devices were required to comply with EU MDR beginning in May 2021.

81fafebc-fd56-44bd-b1ec-80bb755b19e3.label: text_chunk
81fafebc-fd56-44bd-b1ec-80bb755b19e3.text:
The Company considers the adoption of EU MDR to be a significant one-time regulatory change and is not indicative of on-going operations.

  The Company has excluded only external third-party regulatory and consulting costs from its MedTech operating segments' measures of profit and loss used for making operating decisions and assessing performance which is expected to be completed during 2024.The tax impact related to special item adjustments reflects the current and deferred income taxes associated with the above pre-tax special items in arriving at adjusted earnings.

properties.relationship_description: The sales decline reported in the fourth quarter of 2022 was primarily driven by reduced sales of the COVID-19 Vaccine.
Acquisition is often followed by Integration, as the acquired entity needs to be integrated into the existing business operations for effective management.

properties.entity_description: Represents the total sales revenue reported for the fourth quarter of 2022, indicating the financial performance of the company.
Represents the earnings per share (EPS) reported for the fourth quarter of 2022, a key indicator of a company's profitability.

properties.relationship_description: The $23.7 Billion figure represents the total sales reported for the fourth quarter of 2022.
Johnson & Johnson is involved in the reconciliation of Non-GAAP financial measures, indicating its financial performance and reporting practices.
The term 'Adjusted EPS' is classified as a non-GAAP financial measure, indicating that it is a specific type of financial metric that does not conform to standard accounting principles.
Contact lenses are sold in the US market, indicating a commercial relationship between the product and the geographical region.
New product research and development is closely related to patents, as securing patents is often a crucial step in protecting innovations developed during this process.
The Company is responsible for the development and manufacturing of the COVID-19 Vaccine, indicating a direct relationship between the organization and the vaccine product.
INTERVENTIONAL SOLUTIONS US is a business unit that operates within the MEDTECH SEGMENT, focusing on medical technology solutions.
Certain prior year amounts have been reclassified to conform to current year product disclosures, suggesting a direct relationship between historical financial data and current reporting standards.
Johnson & Johnson owns and controls its subsidiaries, which operate under its brand and contribute to its overall business operations.

properties.entity_description: New Brunswick is a city in New Jersey, known for being the headquarters of Johnson & Johnson and its historical significance in the pharmaceutical industry.

The relationships outlined involve key financial metrics used to evaluate a company's profitability and leverage. 

1. **Earnings Per Share (EPS)** and **Diluted EPS**: Both are critical metrics for assessing a company's profitability. Diluted EPS offers a more conservative perspective by factoring in potential dilution from convertible securities, thus providing a clearer picture of earnings available to shareholders.

2. **Net Debt**: This financial metric is essential for calculating the **Net Debt-to-Adjusted EBITDA Ratio**, which measures a company's leverage relative to its earnings. Net debt serves as the total debt figure necessary for this assessment, highlighting the company's financial health and ability to manage its obligations.

In summary, these relationships emphasize the importance of EPS and diluted EPS in profitability analysis, alongside net debt's role in evaluating financial leverage through the net debt-to-adjusted EBITDA ratio.

community_summary.270:
The Internal Revenue Service (IRS) plays a crucial role in the oversight of federal tax compliance through two primary functions: audits and enforcement. 

1. **Audits**: The IRS conducts audits of federal tax returns to ensure that individuals and entities comply with tax laws and regulations. This process is essential for maintaining the integrity of the tax system and verifying that taxpayers accurately report their income and pay the appropriate taxes.

2. **Enforcement**: The IRS is also tasked with enforcing federal tax law. This involves ensuring compliance with tax regulations and the collection of taxes as required by law. The enforcement activities are vital for upholding the legal framework governing taxation in the United States.

Overall, the IRS's responsibilities in auditing and enforcing tax laws are integral to the functioning of the federal tax system, ensuring that all taxpayers meet their obligations.

12. **Impact on Financial Performance Indicators**: The revenue performance in fiscal 2022 was expected to influence the pricing and issuance of stock options, linking financial performance to compensation strategies.

Overall, fiscal 2022 was characterized by a mix of challenges and positive developments, with key metrics and financial activities shaping the company's performance and strategic direction.

community_summary.386:
The relationships outlined in the knowledge graph highlight the interactions between various financial entities and their implications for dividend payments and creditor priorities.

1. **Preference Stock and Common Stock**: The relationship indicates that the payment of dividends on Common Stock is contingent upon the declaration and payment of full cumulative dividends on outstanding Preference Stock. This establishes a hierarchy in dividend payments, where Preference Stock holders are prioritized over Common Stock holders.

2. **Priority and Common Stock**: Similar to the first relationship, it reiterates that Common Stock dividends are subject to the prior payment of dividends on Preference Stock, emphasizing the importance of Preference Stock in the dividend distribution process.

3. **Priority and Subsidiaries**: Creditors of subsidiaries, including trade creditors and preferred equity owners, have a priority claim on the assets and earnings of the subsidiary. This means that these creditors are prioritized over those holding claims against the parent company or other financial instruments like Notes.

4. **Subsidiaries and Undistributed Earnings**: Subsidiaries are capable of generating undistributed earnings, which are profits retained within the subsidiary rather than being distributed to the parent company or its shareholders. This highlights the financial autonomy of subsidiaries in managing their profits.

5. **Generate and Undistributed Earnings**: This relationship reinforces the idea that subsidiaries can create undistributed earnings, emphasizing their role in profit retention and financial management separate from the parent company.

In summary, the relationships illustrate a structured financial hierarchy where Preference Stock holders have priority in dividend payments over Common Stock holders, and subsidiary creditors have precedence over other claims. Additionally, subsidiaries play a crucial role in generating and retaining profits, which can impact the overall financial strategy of the parent company.

The relationships outlined highlight the connections between key financial metrics and their implications for a company's performance:

1. **EBITDA and Euribor**: EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, and Amortization) can be influenced by interest rates, specifically the Euribor rate. Changes in Euribor can impact a company's earnings and overall financial performance, indicating that interest rates play a significant role in shaping EBITDA.

2. **EBITDA and Net Earnings**: EBITDA serves as a critical measure of a company's operational performance. community_summary.482:
### Summary of Relationships Involving Notes

1. **Due Date**: The Notes are due on **February 15, 2024**, marking the maturity date for the associated financial instrument.

2. **Currency Type**: The Notes are **Euro-denominated**, indicating that all financial obligations are expressed in euros.

3. **Governing Document**: The terms and conditions of the Notes are governed by an **Indenture**, which establishes the legal framework for their issuance and management.

4. **Ranking and Subordination**: The Notes rank equally with all existing and future **unsecured and unsubordinated indebtedness**. However, they are effectively **subordinated to the claims of creditors of subsidiaries**, meaning in liquidation scenarios, subsidiary creditors have priority over Notes holders.

5. **Redemption Procedures**: Holders must receive notice **15 to 45 days** prior to the redemption date. The Notes can be redeemed by the issuer at their option, and they are subject to redemption prior to maturity under certain **United States taxation** events.

6. **Minimum Principal Amount**: The minimum principal amount of the Notes that must remain outstanding after a partial redemption is **€100,000**, and they can be repurchased in increments of **€1,000**.

7. **Repurchase Obligations**: A **Change of Control Triggering Event** necessitates an obligation to offer to repurchase the Notes from holders, and a third party may fulfill obligations related to this repurchase.

8. **Payment Management**: A **paying agent** is responsible for managing the payment process for the Notes, ensuring proper payment to holders.

9. **Sinking Fund**: The Notes are **not subject to any sinking fund**, meaning they do not benefit from a fund set aside for their repayment.

10. **Default Conditions**: Default can arise from financial transactions such as mergers or asset sales, impacting the obligations tied to the Notes.

11. **Modifications**: Various modifications related to the terms of payment and principal of the Notes can occur, directly affecting the Notes themselves.

12. **Trust Arrangements**: If funds are deposited in trust for payment, the Notes will not be eligible to vote, indicating a relationship with trust financial arrangements.

13. **Representation and Settlement**: The Notes are represented by fully registered global notes managed through **Clearstream**, which also facilitates the settlement of book-entry interests in the Notes.

This summary encapsulates the critical aspects of the relationships involving the Notes, highlighting their financial structure, obligations, and procedural requirements.

1. **Other Comprehensive Income and Amortization of Losses**: Amortization of losses can significantly influence other comprehensive income by including adjustments related to prior service costs.

2. **Brand Recognition and Loyalty**: There is a direct influence of brand recognition on consumer loyalty, where higher recognition typically results in increased loyalty, as consumers are more inclined to repurchase familiar brands.

3. **Profitability and Competitive Position**: Profitability plays a crucial role in shaping a company's competitive position. A more profitable company is better equipped to invest in growth, marketing, and innovation, thereby enhancing its market standing.

4. **Local Economic Conditions and Consumer Demand**: Local economic conditions are pivotal in influencing consumer demand, as fluctuations in income and employment levels directly affect purchasing behaviors.

5. **Economic Factors and Demand**: Broader economic factors, including market stability and competition, significantly impact consumer demand for various products and services.

6. **Health Care Inflation and Retirees' Benefits**: Health care inflation directly affects retirees' benefits by determining necessary adjustments to keep these benefits aligned with rising health care costs.

7. **Financial Performance and Future Results**: A company's financial performance is a key indicator of its future results, reflecting its current health and potential for growth or decline.

These relationships illustrate the interconnectedness of economic indicators, consumer behavior, and corporate performance, emphasizing how changes in one area can ripple through to affect others.

The relationships outlined involve key financial concepts and their representation in a company's financial statements:

1. **Translation Adjustments and Accumulated Other Comprehensive Loss (AOCI)**: Translation adjustments are recorded in AOCI to reflect the impact of currency fluctuations on the company's financial statements. This indicates that changes in currency values are accounted for in a specific section of the financial reports, highlighting their significance in assessing the company's overall financial health.

2. **Periodic Exit Costs and Consolidated Balance Sheets**: Periodic exit costs are reflected in the consolidated balance sheets, as they represent liabilities that influence the company's overall financial position. This relationship emphasizes the importance of recognizing these costs in financial reporting, as they can affect the company's liabilities and, consequently, its financial stability.

In summary, the relationships illustrate how translation adjustments and periodic exit costs are crucial components in financial reporting, impacting AOCI and consolidated balance sheets, respectively. These elements are essential for providing a clear picture of a company's financial status and the effects of external factors like currency fluctuations and exit liabilities.

The relationships summarized involve the concepts of Fair Value, Carrying Amount, and Discounted Cash Flows, highlighting their interconnections in financial valuation:

1. **Fair Value and Carrying Amount**: Fair Value is compared to Carrying Amount to assess whether an impairment loss exists. This relationship underscores the importance of these financial metrics in evaluating asset value and potential losses.

2. **Fair Value and Discounted Cash Flows**: Fair Value is estimated using Discounted Cash Flows, particularly for long-term debt when market prices are unavailable. This indicates a methodological approach to determining fair value based on future cash flow projections.

Overall, these relationships illustrate how Fair Value serves as a critical measure in financial reporting, relying on comparisons with Carrying Amount and estimation through Discounted Cash Flows to ensure accurate asset valuation.

community_summary.697:
### Summary of Relationships

1. **Impact on Sustainability Measures**: Organizations implement sustainability measures to reduce their carbon footprint, aiming to lower greenhouse gas emissions and enhance environmental performance.

2. **Impact on Input Costs and Transaction Costs**: Higher input costs directly lead to increased transaction costs, indicating a reciprocal relationship where fluctuations in one affect the other.

3. **Impact of Material Contractual Obligations on Cash Flows**: Material contractual obligations can significantly influence cash flows, as they entail commitments that necessitate cash outflows, thereby affecting liquidity.

4. **Impact of Actuarial Net Losses on Postretirement Benefit Expenses**: Actuarial net losses contribute to future increases in postretirement benefit expenses, reflecting the company's financial obligations towards employee benefits after retirement.

5. **Impact of Transaction Costs on Diluted Earnings Per Share**: Transaction costs reduce diluted earnings per share since they are deducted from earnings, thus diminishing the amount available to shareholders.

6. **Impact of Foreign Currency Exchange Rates on Adjusted Operating Profit**: Fluctuations in foreign currency exchange rates can alter the adjusted operating profit by affecting the value of revenues and expenses in foreign currencies.

7. **Impact of Legal and Regulatory Environment on Tax Legislation**: Changes in tax legislation, as part of the legal and regulatory environment, directly influence taxation policies that businesses must adhere to.

8. **Impact of Foreign Economic Conditions on Market Value of Derivatives**: Foreign economic conditions, including currency rate fluctuations, can significantly affect the market value of derivatives used for managing price risk.

9. **Impact of Divestitures on Selling, General, and Administrative Expenses**: Divestitures can lead to a reduction in selling, general, and administrative expenses by decreasing overhead costs associated with sold business units.

10. **Impact of Stock Compensation Plans on Paid-in Capital**: Stock compensation plans can modify the paid-in capital, affecting the company's capital structure and overall financial metrics.

11. **Impact of ARPA on CARES Act**: The American Rescue Plan Act (ARPA) is linked to financial results and may be influenced by or impact the provisions of the CARES Act.

12. **Impact of State Tax Benefits on U.S. Federal Income Taxes**: State tax benefits can reduce federal income tax liabilities, demonstrating a direct relationship between state and federal tax frameworks.

13. **Impact of Inventory Values on Reserve for LIFO Valuation**: The reserve for LIFO (Last In, First Out) valuation is influenced by changes in inventory values, which in turn affects overall financial reporting metrics. 

This summary encapsulates the interconnected nature of various financial and operational factors, highlighting how changes in one area can significantly impact others within an organization.
